title: An entire Herculaneum scroll has been read for the first time
summary:
On June 25th, 2026, researchers announced a monumental breakthrough: an entire Herculaneum scroll, PHerc. 1667, has been virtually unwrapped and read from beginning to end without being physically opened. Sealed since the Vesuvius eruption in 79 AD, these fragile, carbonized scrolls were previously unreadable without destruction.

The innovative method involved high-resolution X-ray microtomography, 3D reconstruction to map the wound papyrus, flattening the surface, and machine learning to detect faint ink traces. The text of PHerc. 1667, approximately 1.4 meters long with twenty-two columns of Greek, is identified as a Stoic philosophical treatise on ethics, dating to the 2nd century BC, referencing Aristocreon. This marks the first continuous, end-to-end reading of such a scroll.

The research also yielded successes with two other scrolls: PHerc. Paris 4 (Scroll 1) where higher-resolution imaging made ink directly visible in 3D X-ray data, independently confirming previous readings; and PHerc. 139, revealing its title and author as Philodemus, *On Gods, Book 8*.

This achievement is a testament to open science, spearheaded by Professor Brent Seales's EduceLab and significantly advanced by the Vesuvius Challenge, a global, donation-funded community effort. All data, code, and findings are openly available, fostering continued research. This scalable method promises to unlock an entire library of ancient philosophy, poetry, and prose from the hundreds of remaining sealed scrolls.
chinese_title: 赫库兰尼姆的一整卷卷轴首次被解读。
chinese_summary:
2026年6月25日，研究人员宣布了一项里程碑式的突破：整卷赫库兰尼姆纸莎草卷轴PHerc. 1667，在未被物理展开的情况下，已被虚拟展开并从头到尾阅读。这些脆弱的碳化卷轴自公元79年维苏威火山喷发以来一直密封，此前若不损毁则无法阅读。

这种创新方法包括高分辨率X射线显微断层扫描技术、三维重建以绘制卷绕的纸莎草地图、展平表面，以及利用机器学习检测微弱的墨迹。PHerc. 1667的文本，长约1.4米，包含22栏希腊文，被确认为一篇关于伦理的斯多葛哲学论著，可追溯到公元前2世纪，其中提及了亚里斯托克里翁。这标志着首次对这类卷轴进行连续的、从头到尾的阅读。

这项研究还在另外两卷卷轴上取得了成功：PHerc. Paris 4（卷轴1），其中更高分辨率的成像使得墨迹在3D X射线数据中直接可见，独立证实了此前的阅读结果；以及PHerc. 139，揭示其标题和作者为菲洛德摩斯（Philodemus）的《论诸神，第八卷》（*On Gods, Book 8*）。

这一成就证明了开放科学的力量，由布伦特·希尔斯教授（Professor Brent Seales）的EduceLab率先启动，并由“维苏威挑战赛”（Vesuvius Challenge）——一项全球性的、由捐赠资助的社区项目——大力推进。所有数据、代码和研究结果均已公开，以促进持续研究。这种可扩展的方法有望从数百卷剩余的密封卷轴中，解开一整座古代哲学、诗歌和散文的图书馆。

title: Om Malik has died
summary: The article's primary and sole piece of information is the announcement of Om Malik's death, stated directly in the title. Beyond this, the provided content does not offer any further details regarding the death itself. Instead, it consists of generic website prompts for user interaction, such as an invitation to share links, a "Loading..." message, and a "Reply" option, suggesting the text is an excerpt from a discussion forum or blog post.
chinese_title: 欧姆·马利克已去世
chinese_summary: 这篇文章最主要也是唯一的信息是Om Malik去世的公告，直接在标题中指出。除此之外，所提供的内容没有提供关于死亡本身的任何进一步细节。相反，它由常见的网站用户互动提示组成，例如分享链接的邀请、一个“加载中…”的消息和一个“回复”选项，这表明这段文字摘自一个讨论论坛或博客文章。

title: The 'papers, please' era of the internet will decimate your privacy
summary:
The article warns that the internet's emerging "papers, please" era, characterized by mandatory age verification, will decimate user privacy and freedom of speech by effectively forcing identity verification. It outlines a scenario where users must provide sensitive personal data, like government IDs or biometric information, to unknown third-party apps for online access, raising concerns about data breaches, foreign government access, and hacking.

Australia's 2025 under-16 social media ban exemplifies this trend. Despite its implementation, the law has largely failed to curb underage social media use. It mandates platforms collect extensive user data, often via third-party services, leading to significant privacy risks. A real-world breach affecting 70,000 Australians, involving government IDs linked to age assurance, underscores these dangers. The author notes this system necessitates user profiling, eroding online anonymity.

The UK is pursuing an even stricter "Australia-plus" model, signaling more severe enforcement and controversially considering targeting VPNs, mirroring restrictive regimes. Alarmingly, the United States is following suit with numerous state-level age verification laws and federal proposals like the Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA), which would mandate identity verification across platforms nationwide.

The author concludes that while protecting children online is crucial, these policies create an "intolerable burden" on free and anonymous speech. This pervasive legislative infrastructure of surveillance, once established, will be difficult to dismantle, leading to self-censorship and severe First Amendment violations.
chinese_title: 互联网的“请出示证件”时代将摧毁你的隐私。
chinese_summary:
文章警告称，互联网正在出现的以强制性年龄验证为特点的“请出示证件”时代，将通过有效强制身份验证的方式，严重损害用户隐私和言论自由。文章描绘了一个场景：用户必须向未知的第三方应用程序提供敏感的个人数据，例如政府身份证件或生物识别信息，以获取在线访问权限，这引发了对数据泄露、外国政府获取信息和黑客攻击的担忧。

澳大利亚2025年针对16岁以下人群的社交媒体禁令是这一趋势的例证。尽管该法律已经实施，但它在很大程度上未能遏制未成年人使用社交媒体。该法律强制平台收集大量的用户数据，通常通过第三方服务进行，从而带来严重的隐私风险。一起影响了7万澳大利亚人的真实数据泄露事件，其中涉及与年龄验证相关的政府身份证件，凸显了这些危险。作者指出，这一系统必然导致用户画像的建立，侵蚀了在线匿名性。

英国正在推行一种更为严格的“澳大利亚增强版”模式，预示着更严厉的执法，并引发争议地考虑针对VPN进行限制，效仿了限制性政权的做法。令人担忧的是，美国也正在效仿，出台了多项州级年龄验证法律，以及诸如《儿童在线安全法案》(KOSA)等联邦提案，该法案将强制要求全国范围内的平台进行身份验证。

作者总结道，尽管保护儿童在线安全至关重要，但这些政策给自由和匿名的言论带来了“无法承受的负担”。这种普遍存在的监控立法基础设施一旦建立，将难以拆除，从而导致自我审查和对第一修正案的严重侵犯。

title: Show HN: I made Google Trends for Hacker News by indexing 18 years of comments
summary:
"Hacker News Trends" is a new tool that indexes 18 years of Hacker News comments to visualize the popularity and evolution of any topic, tool, or person over time. Built on Upstash Redis Search, it allows users to search and chart how frequently terms appear across 45 million posts and comments, overlaying multiple terms to observe their traction, rise, and fall. Users can also click through to the actual discussions behind the trends.

The platform showcases numerous "Popular Comparisons" demonstrating its utility, such as the rivalry between Vercel and Cloudflare, the evolution of database dominance between MySQL and Postgres, and the succession of ML frameworks like TensorFlow, PyTorch, and JAX.

Beyond specific comparisons, the tool categorizes trends into comprehensive sections including:
*   **People:** Influential figures (Elon Musk, Sam Altman).
*   **AI & LLMs:** Tracking advancements like ChatGPT, Llama, Stable Diffusion.
*   **Products & Hardware:** From Apple Silicon to Vision Pro.
*   **Languages & Dev Tools:** Trends in Zig, Bun, TypeScript.
*   **JS Frameworks:** The progression from jQuery to React, Svelte, HTMX.
*   **Startups & Companies:** Figma, Substack, Reddit.
*   **Cloud & Hosting:** AWS, Heroku, Vercel.
*   **Security Incidents:** Log4j, Heartbleed.
*   **Crypto & Hype Cycles:** Bitcoin, NFTs, Web3.
*   **Internet & Culture:** Mastodon, Fediverse.
*   **Dev Culture:** Monorepos, TDD, Microservices.
*   **Industry Zeitgeist:** Layoffs, AI bubble, Quiet Quitting.
*   **Science & Frontier Tech:** LK-99, Fusion energy.
*   **Open-source license wars:** Redis, Terraform.
*   **Gaming:** Unity, Godot.
*   **Health & Longevity:** Ozempic, Intermittent Fasting.

This tool offers a dynamic, historical perspective on the shifting interests and major events within the tech community as chronicled on Hacker News.
chinese_title: 展示 HN: 我制作了 Hacker News 版谷歌趋势，索引了 18 年的评论
chinese_summary:
"Hacker News 趋势"是一款新工具，它索引了 Hacker News 18年来的评论，以可视化任何话题、工具或人物在一段时间内的受欢迎程度和演变。基于 Upstash Redis Search 构建，它允许用户搜索和绘制词条在4500万条帖子和评论中出现的频率图，并叠加多个词条以观察它们的吸引力、兴衰。用户还可以点击查看趋势背后的实际讨论。

该平台展示了许多“热门比较”，以证明其实用性，例如 Vercel 和 Cloudflare 之间的竞争、MySQL 和 Postgres 之间数据库主导地位的演变，以及 TensorFlow、PyTorch 和 JAX 等机器学习框架的更迭。

除了具体的比较之外，该工具还将趋势分为以下综合类别：
*   **人物：** 影响力人物（埃隆·马斯克、萨姆·奥特曼）。
*   **AI 与大型语言模型：** 追踪 ChatGPT、Llama、Stable Diffusion 等进展。
*   **产品与硬件：** 从 Apple Silicon 到 Vision Pro。
*   **语言与开发工具：** Zig、Bun、TypeScript 的趋势。
*   **JS 框架：** 从 jQuery 到 React、Svelte、HTMX 的演进。
*   **初创公司与企业：** Figma、Substack、Reddit。
*   **云与托管：** AWS、Heroku、Vercel。
*   **安全事件：** Log4j、Heartbleed。
*   **加密货币与炒作周期：** 比特币、NFTs、Web3。
*   **互联网与文化：** Mastodon、Fediverse。
*   **开发文化：** Monorepos、TDD、微服务。
*   **行业思潮：** 裁员、AI 泡沫、静默离职。
*   **科学与前沿科技：** LK-99、核聚变能源。
*   **开源许可协议之争：** Redis、Terraform。
*   **游戏：** Unity、Godot。
*   **健康与长寿：** Ozempic、间歇性禁食。

该工具提供了一个动态的、历史性的视角，展现了 Hacker News 上所记录的科技社区中不断变化的兴趣和重大事件。

title: Half-Life 2 in a Browser
summary:
This extremely concise "article" announces a significant development: the classic first-person shooter *Half-Life 2* is being made available or is playable directly within a web browser.

The title, "Half-Life 2 in a Browser," clearly states the core subject. The content, "web hl2Downloading...", further confirms that the game is actively being loaded or accessed through a web-based interface. This brief statement implies a major technical achievement, potentially leveraging advancements in cloud gaming, streaming, or web technologies like WebAssembly, to enable a graphically demanding title to run directly on a web platform without traditional local installation. The article serves as a direct, albeit minimal, confirmation that this ambitious project is underway or functional.
chinese_title: 半条命2 浏览器版
chinese_summary:
这篇极其简洁的“文章”宣布了一项重大进展：经典第一人称射击游戏《半衰期2》正在网页浏览器中直接提供或可玩。

标题“浏览器版《半衰期2》”明确指出了核心主题。内容“web hl2Downloading...”进一步证实了该游戏正通过一个基于网络的界面被主动加载或访问。这一简短的声明暗示了一项重大的技术成就，它可能利用了云游戏、流媒体或WebAssembly等网络技术方面的进步，使得一款对图形要求很高的游戏能够直接在网络平台上运行，而无需传统的本地安装。这篇文章作为一个直接但极简的确认，表明这项雄心勃勃的项目正在进行中或已可运行。

title: Hey Nico, you didn't vibe code your data room but stole it from Papermark
summary:
Marc Seitz (@mfts0) publicly accused Nico Laqua (@nico_laqua) of copyright and license infringement. Seitz alleges that Laqua's data room product was not developed independently ("vibe coded") but was "stolen" from Papermark's open-source and enterprise-licensed code.

Seitz characterized this act as "fraud" rather than agile development ("moving fast and breaking things"), and demanded that Laqua immediately take down the infringing product. He further stated that this incident casts doubt on Laqua's entire business and reflects poorly on the Y Combinator community, tagging @garrytan, @snowmaker, and @ycombinator in the post.
chinese_title: Hey Nico, you didn't vibe code your data room but stole it from Papermark
chinese_summary:
Marc Seitz (@mfts0) 公开指控 Nico Laqua (@nico_laqua) 侵犯版权和许可。Seitz 声称 Laqua 的数据室产品并非独立开发（“凭感觉编码”），而是“窃取”自 Papermark 的开源及企业许可代码。

Seitz 将此行为定性为“欺诈”，而非敏捷开发（“快速行动，打破常规”），并要求 Laqua 立即下架侵权产品。他进一步指出，这一事件让人们对 Laqua 的整个业务产生怀疑，也给 Y Combinator 社区带来了负面影响，并在帖子中标记了 @garrytan、@snowmaker 和 @ycombinator。
